explanations.1: This line checks if the text '--quiet' appears anywhere in ARGS; if it does, QUIET becomes 'true', otherwise 'false'.
explanations.4: This checks if QUIET was set to 'false', and only runs the next line if that condition is true.
explanations.5: The \e[ codes are ANSI escape sequences that color and style terminal output — in this case, making text bold and dark gray.
explanations.7: Similar to line 5, this uses ANSI codes to print a message in bold green; the \n\n adds two newlines for spacing.
